content.problemStatement:
Chef and Chefina are playing a game. There are two piles numbered 111 and 222.

Pile 111 contains XXX stones while Pile 222 contains YYY stones.

Chef starts the game.


In his turn, Chef can either remove 111 stone each from both the piles **or** remove 222 stones from pile 111.
On the other hand, Chefina, in her turn, can either remove 111 stone each from both the piles **or** remove 222 stones from pile 222.
The player who cannot make a move loses. Determine the winner if both players play optimally.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing two integers XXX and YYY, the initial number of stones in piles 111 and 222 respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output `CHEF` if Chef wins the game, and `CHEFINA` otherwise.


You may print each character in uppercase or lowercase. For example, the strings `CHEF`, `chef`, `Chef`, and `cHEf` are all considered identical.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider the following sequence of moves:

Chef removes 111 stone each from both the piles. Thus, pile 111 has no stone left while pile 222 has 999 stones left.
Chefina removes 222 stones from pile 222. Thus, pile 111 has no stones left while pile 222 has 777 stones left.

Since Chef has no valid move, he loses. Note that this might not be an optimal sequence of moves.
Test case 222: Consider the following sequence of moves:

Chef removes 111 stone each from both the piles. Thus, pile 111 has 111 stone left and pile 222 has 111 stone left.
Chefina removes 111 stone each from both the piles. Thus, pile 111 has no stones left and pile 222 has no stones left.

Since Chef has no valid move, he loses. Note that this might not be an optimal sequence of moves.
